concept_tree.learning_objective: Students should be able to define and describe a solution.
Students should be able to identify the components of a solution.
Students should be able to explain the concept of solubility.
Students should be able to describe solid solutions.
Students should be able to describe substitutional solid solutions.

search_terms: smaller atoms occupy voids between larger atoms
concept_tree.learning_objective: Students should be able to describe interstitial solid solutions.

keywords: smaller atoms occupy voids between larger atoms

concept_tree.learning_objective: Students should be able to explain mass percentage.
Students should be able to describe mole fraction.
Students should be able to calculate and interpret molarity (M) of a solution.
Students should be able to understand the concept of molality (m) and its application.
Students should be able to understand the concept of parts per million (ppm) and its application.
Students should be able to understand the concept of vapour pressure and its relation with temperature.
Students should be able to understand Raoult’s Law and its application.
Students should be able to understand the concept of an ideal solution and its properties.
Students should be able to understand the concept of a non-ideal solution and its properties.
Students should be able to understand the concept of an azeotrope and its properties.
Students should be able to explain colligative properties and their dependence on the number of solute particles.
Students should be able to describe the concept of relative lowering of vapour pressure and its relation with solute concentration.
Students should be able to explain the concept of depression in freezing point and its relation with solute concentration.
Students should be able to describe the concept of elevation in boiling point and its relation with solute concentration.
Students should be able to explain the concept of osmotic pressure and its relation with solute concentration.
Students should be able to describe the concept of isotonic solutions and their osmotic pressure.
Students should be able to explain the concept of hypertonic solutions and their osmotic pressure.
Students should be able to describe the concept of hypotonic solutions and their osmotic pressure.
Students should be able to understand the concept of abnormal molar mass in solutions.
Students should be able to explain the concept of degree of association in solution.
Students should be able to describe the concept of degree of dissociation in solution.
Students should be able to apply Henry’s Law in solving problems related to gas solubility.

search_terms: ratio of normal molecular mass to observed molecular mass
concept_tree.learning_objective: Students should be able to understand the concept of Vant Hoff Factor (i) in solution.

keywords: ratio of normal molecular mass to observed molecular mass

question_bank.question: What do you mean by Henry’s Law?

The Henry’s Law constant for oxygen dissolved in water is 4.34×104 atm at 25°C. If the partial pressure of oxygen in air is 0.2 atm, under atmospheric pressure conditions. Calculate the concentration in moles per Litre of dissolved oxygen in water in equilibrium with water air at 25°C.
What is Vant Hoff factor?

Ans. It is the ratio of normal molecular mass to observed molecular mass .It is denoted as ‘i’ i = Theoretical Molar mass / observed molar mass = Observed no. of particles / Actual no. of particles = Observed colligative property/Theoretical colligative property
What is the Vant Hoff factor in K4[Fe(CN)6] and BaCl2 ?
Why the molecular mass becomes abnormal?
Ans. Due to association or dissociation of solute in given solvent.
question_bank.answer: A – A and B – B type interactions are more than A – B type interactions. Positive deviation from ideal behaviour is observed.

question_bank.question: Define molarity?
How molarity is related with percentage and density of solution ?
question_bank.answer: M = P x Percentage of density x 10/molar mass

question_bank.question: What role does the molecular interaction play in the solution of alcohol and water?
What is Vant Hoff factor, how is it related with a. degree of dissociation b. degree of association
Why is NaCl used to clear snow from roads?
Why is the boiling point of solution higher than pure liquid?
Out of 1M and 1m aqueous solution which is more concentrated?
Henry’s law constant for two gases are 21.5 and 49.5 atm, which gas is more soluble?
question_bank.answer: KH is inversely proportional to solubility. Hence gas with KH = 21.5 atm is more soluble.

question_bank.question: Define azeotrope. Give an example of maximum boiling azeotrope.
Calculate the volume of 75% of H2SO4 by weight (d=1.8 gm/ml) required to prepare 1L of 0.2M solution.
question_bank.answer: M1 = P x d x 10 /98 M1 V1 = M2V2 14.5ml

Water is used as coolant in vehicles. In cars, ethylene glycol is used as coolant. In cold countries, water gets frozen so it cannot act as coolant. Ethylene glycol is added to water so that its freezing point is lower and it does not freeze.

At hill station snow fall takes place. Clearing the snow from the road is essential for smooth running of traffic- (a)What is the role of ethylene glycol when added to water? (b)Why does sprinkling of salt help in clearing snow covered roads in hilly areas? Explain the phenomenon involved in the process. (c)How is life of people affected by snow fall?

The solution has a boiling point of 100.42 0C . What mass of glycerol was dissolved to make this solution? Kb for water = 0.512 K Kg mol-1 (hint: Tb = KB x WB x 1000 MB x WA)
Ans. 37.73 g
